sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Dana says sale of off-highway business will make stronger company
feed.summary: Dana Incorporated reported its second-quarter earnings, highlighting the sale of its off-highway business to Allison. This strategic move is intended to make Dana a more focused supplier to the light and commercial vehicle markets and substantially strengthens its balance sheet. The company also announced a $1 billion capital return authorization and expects significant cost savings through 2026.

feed.title: Huntington Ingalls Industries (NYSE:HII) Sets New 1-Year High - Should You Buy?
feed.summary: Huntington Ingalls Industries (NYSE:HII) recently hit a new 52-week high of $396.81, fueled by strong momentum and exceeding analyst revenue expectations with a 16.1% year-over-year increase. Despite the stock outperforming its consensus Hold rating and average price target of $325.75, some analysts have issued "Buy" ratings and the company increased its quarterly dividend. Insider selling has occurred, yet institutional investors hold approximately 90.5% of the company's stock.

feed.title: SPY ETF Daily Update, 1/12/2026
feed.summary: The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) saw a slight gain of 0.04% on Monday morning amidst concerns about the Federal Reserve's independence and a proposal to cap credit card interest rates impacting financial stocks. SPY, which closely tracks the S&P 500 Index, has experienced $1 billion in net outflows over the past five days, though retail sentiment remains positive. According to TipRanks' ETF analyst consensus, SPY maintains a Moderate Buy rating with an average price target of $800.11, implying a 15.23% upside potential.

feed.title: Cohen & Steers names Diana Shieh as private real estate COO
feed.summary: Cohen & Steers, Inc. (NYSE:CNS) has appointed Diana Shieh as Chief Operating Officer and Head of Asset Management for its Private Real Estate Group. Shieh brings over 20 years of real estate investment and operational experience, previously serving in senior roles at Oxford Properties Group and Madison International Realty. Her new role will focus on aligning operations and overseeing strategic asset management of the firm's private real estate portfolio.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Adjusts Price Target on Royal Caribbean Group to $373 From $316, Maintains Overweight Rating
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has increased its price target for Royal Caribbean Group (RCL) from $316 to $373, while reiterating an "Overweight" rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects Wells Fargo's continued positive outlook on the cruise line operator. The article also provides a summary of recent news and analyst actions concerning Royal Caribbean Group.

feed.title: Invesco stock hits 52-week high at 28.64 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Invesco Ltd. (IVZ) stock has reached a new 52-week high of $28.64, marking an 82.36% increase over the past year. The asset management firm, with a market capitalization of $12.73 billion, offers a 2.94% dividend yield and has consistently paid dividends for 19 years. This milestone reflects investor confidence and strong performance, ahead of its upcoming earnings report.

feed.title: Boston Scientific to acquire California bladder dysfunction treatment company
feed.summary: Boston Scientific is set to acquire Valencia Technologies, a California-based company specializing in treatments for bladder dysfunction. This acquisition will give Boston Scientific ownership of Valencia Technologies’ eCoin System, an implantable device designed to treat urinary incontinence by stimulating the tibial nerve. The deal is expected to finalize in the first half of the year, further expanding Boston Scientific's urology business.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs Adjusts Price Target on Moderna to $36 From $33, Maintains Neutral Rating
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs has increased its price target for Moderna (MRNA) shares from $33 to $36, while keeping a Neutral rating on the stock. This adjustment comes amidst other analyst actions on Moderna, including a recent downgrade by UBS and price target changes by BofA. The article also provides recent news headlines related to Moderna's revenue outlook for 2025 and ongoing patent disputes.

feed.title: Henry Schein names Frederick M. Lowery as new CEO
feed.summary: Henry Schein Inc., Long Island's largest public company by revenue, has appointed Frederick M. Lowery as its next CEO, replacing longtime leader Stanley M. Bergman. Lowery will assume the role on March 2, while Bergman will transition to chairman of the board to ensure a smooth leadership transition. Lowery brings over 20 years of experience in the healthcare industry, having previously held senior leadership positions at Thermo Fisher Scientific.

feed.title: BMO Capital Adjusts Price Target on EOG Resources to $120 From $126, Maintains Outperform Rating
feed.summary: BMO Capital has reduced its price target for EOG Resources (EOG) to $120 from $126, while concurrently reiterating an "Outperform" rating on the stock. This adjustment comes amidst a series of analyst rating changes for EOG Resources, reflecting varying outlooks on the oil and gas exploration and production company. Despite the revised price target, BMO Capital continues to recommend EOG Resources as an "Outperform" investment.

feed.title: Raymond James and Boys & Girls Clubs of America Announce
feed.summary: Raymond James and Boys & Girls Clubs of America are partnering in a three-year, $10 million initiative to advance academic success and leadership development for young people. The collaboration will fund academic programs like Project Learn and Summer Brain Gain, engage Raymond James employees as volunteers, and support the Youth of the Year program. This partnership aims to equip youth with crucial tools and mentorship for their future in and out of the classroom.

feed.title: Man Sues McDonald’s After Vagrant Fatally Throws Plaintiff’s Wife to Pavement
feed.summary: A man is suing McDonald's and two franchise holders for wrongful death, negligence, and emotional distress after his wife was fatally injured by a vagrant at a Boyle Heights drive-through in March 2024. The lawsuit alleges that the restaurant was notorious for safety issues and that employees failed to intervene or call 911 despite the vagrant's history of loitering and an ongoing confrontation. The plaintiff claims that reasonable action by the defendants could have prevented his wife's death.

feed.title: Mizuho Issues Positive Forecast for Mid-America Apartment Communities (NYSE:MAA) Stock Price
feed.summary: Mizuho has raised its price target for Mid-America Apartment Communities (NYSE:MAA) to $150 from $146, maintaining an "outperform" rating, which suggests a 10.9% upside. This positive forecast comes despite a mixed analyst landscape, with a consensus "Hold" rating and a target of $155.37, as other firms recently downgraded or cut targets for MAA. The company reported quarterly EPS of $2.16, missing estimates slightly, and provided FY2025 guidance of $8.68–$8.80 EPS.

feed.title: Mizuho Raises Price Target for Albemarle (ALB) to $156.00 | ALB Stock News
feed.summary: Mizuho analyst John Roberts has raised the price target for Albemarle (ALB) to $156.00 from $132.00, while maintaining a 'Neutral' rating. This adjustment reflects an 18.18% increase and follows other recent analyst adjustments, with the average target price from 23 analysts now at $138.61. Albemarle, a major lithium producer for EV batteries and bromine producer for flame retardants, continues to be a focus for market analysts.

feed.title: Apple Inc. (AAPL) Wedbush Stays Bullish with $350 Target Update
feed.summary: Wedbush reiterated its "Outperform" rating for Apple Inc. (AAPL) and maintained a 12-month price target of $350.00 per share. The firm's bullish outlook is driven by Apple's services ecosystem, strong iPhone demand, and potential benefits from AI adoption and hardware refresh cycles. Despite macroeconomic factors, Wedbush believes Apple's brand strength and financial flexibility position it for long-term outperformance.

feed.title: Henry Schein appoints industry veteran Frederick M. Lowery as new CEO
feed.summary: Henry Schein has appointed Frederick M. Lowery as its new chief executive officer, effective March 2. Lowery brings over 20 years of healthcare distribution and operational leadership experience, including his most recent role at Thermo Fisher Scientific. He succeeds Stanley M. Bergman, who will continue as chairman of the board after 35 years as CEO.

feed.title: What General Dynamics (GD)'s Reinvestment-Focused $1.5 Trillion U.S. Defense Proposal Means For Shareholders
feed.summary: President Trump's recent proposal to increase the 2027 U.S. defense budget to $1.50 trillion and tie defense contractor contracts and CEO pay to reinvestment in weapons production could significantly impact General Dynamics (GD). This policy aims to redirect cash flow from dividends and stock buybacks towards growth projects like submarine and combat systems programs, despite the company's consistent dividend history. While the increased budget acts as a near-term catalyst for growth funding, the new reinvestment rules may constrain how cash is returned to shareholders, potentially leading to more investment in core defense technologies.

feed.title: Expeditors International of Washington (NASDAQ:EXPD) Hits New 52-Week High on Analyst Upgrade
feed.summary: Expeditors International of Washington (NASDAQ:EXPD) achieved a new 52-week high after Bank of America upgraded its stock from Neutral to Buy. Despite the upgrade, analyst coverage remains mixed with an average "Hold" rating and a target price of $128.80. While corporate insiders have been selling shares, institutional investors hold a significant majority of the company's stock.

feed.title: Apple’s TSMC Dominance Wanes as AI Demand from Nvidia, AMD Drives Revenue Surge
feed.summary: Apple's long-standing dominance over TSMC is diminishing as the surging demand for AI chips from companies like Nvidia and AMD reshapes the semiconductor manufacturing landscape. TSMC's Q4 2025 revenue growth, driven primarily by AI processor orders, indicates a significant power shift. While Apple continues to reserve advanced nodes and ramp up its own AI efforts, the diversification of TSMC's client base means less leverage for Apple and a more competitive environment for cutting-edge technology.

feed.title: Top Wall Street Forecasters Revamp Wells Fargo Expectations Ahead Of Q4 Earnings
feed.summary: Wall Street analysts have updated their expectations for Wells Fargo (NYSE: WFC) ahead of its fourth-quarter earnings release, with forecasts indicating increased earnings per share and revenue. Several analysts from firms like TD Cowen, Truist Securities, Baird, Barclays, and Keefe, Bruyette & Woods have adjusted their ratings and price targets for WFC, reflecting varying outlooks. Wells Fargo also announced a decrease in its prime rate from 7.00% to 6.75%.

feed.title: Google Confirms Multiyear AI Deal to Power Apple Models, Siri
feed.summary: Google has confirmed a multiyear agreement with Apple to provide AI technology for Apple's models and Siri. This partnership comes after Apple's evaluation determined Google's AI offers the most capable foundation for its Apple Foundation Models, promising new innovative experiences for users. The specific terms of this deal have not been revealed.

feed.title: What’s Happening With Super Micro Stock?
feed.summary: Super Micro Computer (SMCI) has experienced significant volatility despite being a major beneficiary of the AI boom. The stock, nearly flat over the past year, faces mounting competition from Dell and HPE, which is pressuring its margins. However, SMCI maintains competitive advantages through its leadership in direct liquid cooling for energy efficiency and its rapid deployment of new Nvidia platforms.

feed.title: Kroger Sells Vitacost, Scales Generative AI For Improved Customer Experience
feed.summary: Kroger has sold its health and wellness e-commerce subsidiary Vitacost to iHerb as part of its strategy to focus on core assets. Concurrently, the retailer is intensifying its use of generative AI through an expanded collaboration with Google Cloud to create an advanced personal shopping assistant, aiming to significantly enhance the customer experience with integrated meal planning, faster shopping, and proactive issue resolution. This initiative leverages Gemini Enterprise for CX to deliver a more personalized and efficient grocery shopping journey.

feed.title: Why Moderna Stock Is Dropping After Good News
feed.summary: Moderna pre-announced revenue expectations for 2025, projecting around $1.9 billion, which is above the midpoint of its previous guidance. Despite this seemingly positive news, the company's stock experienced a decline. This article explores the reasons behind this counterintuitive market reaction.

feed.title: Donegal reports Q4 and full-year 2024 results
feed.summary: Donegal Insurance Group reported strong financial results for both Q4 and the full-year 2024, showing significant improvements in net income and combined ratio. The company attributed this success to premium growth, underwriting enhancements, and cost reduction efforts. CEO Kevin G. Burke emphasized ongoing strategies including targeted rate increases and new business development to sustain growth and boost shareholder value.

feed.title: Mizuho Securities Adjusts American Tower Price Target to $189 From $217, Maintains Neutral Rating
feed.summary: Mizuho Securities has lowered its price target for American Tower (NYSE:AMT) to $189 from $217, while maintaining a Neutral rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a recent trend of several analysts revising their price targets and ratings for American Tower in January, with some downgrading the stock and others maintaining or upgrading their recommendations. The article also provides key financial data and a company profile for American Tower Corporation.

feed.title: Capital Wealth Management LLC Boosts Stake in GraniteShares Bloomberg Commodity Broad Strategy No K-1 ETF (NYSEARCA:COMB)
feed.summary: Capital Wealth Management LLC significantly increased its holdings in the GraniteShares Bloomberg Commodity Broad Strategy No K-1 ETF (NYSEARCA:COMB) by 41.7% during the third quarter, making it their 23rd largest investment. Several other hedge funds also adjusted their positions in COMB, with some acquiring new stakes and others boosting existing ones. The article also provides a brief overview of the ETF's recent stock performance.

feed.title: Biogen says co received European Commission approval for high dose regimen of Spinraza
feed.summary: Biogen announced that the European Commission has approved a high-dose regimen of its drug Spinraza for the treatment of spinal muscular atrophy. This approval marks a significant development for patients in Europe who may benefit from this updated dosing strategy. The news was published on January 12, 2026, at 11:25 am EST, according to Reuters.

feed.title: Mizuho Securities Adjusts Phillips 66 Price Target to $152 From $150, Maintains Neutral Rating
feed.summary: Mizuho Securities has increased its price target for Phillips 66 (PSX) to $152 from $150, while reiterating a Neutral rating on the stock. This adjustment comes amidst other analyst actions, with Piper Sandler also recently adjusting their price target for Phillips 66 to $153 from $155, maintaining a Neutral rating. Phillips 66 is a diversified energy company primarily involved in petroleum product trading and distribution, refining, and the processing, transportation, and storage of petroleum products and natural gas.

feed.title: Ameriprise Financial : Experienced Team With $120 Million in Assets Joins Ameriprise Financial for Enhanced Client Experience and Operational Efficiency
feed.summary: Loveday Caruso Wealth Management Group, an advisory practice with $120 million in assets, has joined Ameriprise Financial's independent channel from LPL Financial. The team cited Ameriprise's client-centered technology, practice management efficiency, and strong home office support as key reasons for the move. They anticipate accelerated business growth and improved client engagement through Ameriprise's resources.

feed.title: Invesco stock hits 52-week high at 28.64 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Invesco (IVZ) stock reached a new 52-week high of $28.64, reflecting an 82.36% increase over the past year. The asset management firm, with a market capitalization of $12.73 billion, offers a 2.94% dividend yield and has maintained payouts for 19 consecutive years. Recent developments include a slight decrease in assets under management, a restructuring of the Invesco QQQ Trust, and a raised price target from BMO Capital.

feed.title: Borgwarner stock hits 52-week high at $48.19 By Investing.com
feed.summary: BorgWarner Inc. stock recently reached a new 52-week high of $48.19, reflecting a 55.89% increase over the past year and a 35.7% gain in the last six months. This surge is attributed to the company's strategic focus on innovation and electric vehicle components, positioning it favorably in the automotive sector. Despite trading slightly below its Fair Value, BorgWarner maintains a "GOOD" financial health score and has consistently paid dividends for 13 years.

feed.title: Assessing Enphase Energy (ENPH) Valuation After IQ9N-3P Launch And Earnings Beat
feed.summary: Enphase Energy caught attention after shipping its IQ9N-3P Commercial Microinverter and exceeding recent earnings estimates. While short-term stock momentum is positive, longer-term holders are still under pressure, with the stock down significantly over the past year. The article explores differing valuation perspectives, with one narrative suggesting the stock is 8.4% undervalued, while a DCF model indicates it might be overvalued.

feed.title: Applied Materials stock rises on Susquehanna upgrade as chip gear spending call widens
feed.summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) shares rose 1.7% in early trading following an upgrade from Susquehanna to "Positive" with a price target increase from $180 to $400. The upgrade is based on anticipated increased spending in wafer fab equipment (WFE), with projections of $120 billion in current spending and potential for over $150 billion. Investors are now looking forward to upcoming CPI data and TSMC's earnings call for further market signals.

feed.title: A Look At Wells Fargo (WFC) Valuation As Investors Position For Upcoming Earnings
feed.summary: Wells Fargo (WFC) is gaining investor attention ahead of its January 14 earnings report, with its stock price at US$95.95, reflecting a 39.0% total shareholder return over the past year. Analysts generally consider WFC to be undervalued at around US$100.00, despite its P/E ratio of 15.1x being above the US banks industry average. The article emphasizes that while the valuation suggests a buying opportunity, investors must consider ongoing regulatory challenges and competition.

feed.title: Alphabet Becomes Newest $4 Trillion Company, Joining Nvidia and Apple
feed.summary: Alphabet's market capitalization surpassed $4 trillion in intraday trading, making it the latest tech giant to reach this milestone. This achievement is attributed to investor optimism surrounding its growing artificial intelligence business and the recent launch of its Gemini 3 AI model. Shares of Alphabet rose to a record $334.04 on Monday morning, reflecting strong growth of 65.4% last year.

feed.title: Netflix had a huge night at the 2026 Golden Globes with 7 wins
feed.summary: Netflix dominated the 2026 Golden Globes with seven wins, significantly ahead of other streaming platforms. Their hit series "Adolescence" secured four awards, and "KPop Demon Hunters" won best animated feature and best original song. This success comes as Netflix prepares for its acquisition of Warner Bros., highlighting its growing industry dominance.

feed.title: Mizuho Lowers Price Target for Digital Realty Trust (DLR) to $18
feed.summary: Mizuho has adjusted its price target for Digital Realty Trust (DLR) downward from $191.00 to $180.00, while maintaining an "Outperform" rating. This 5.76% decrease reflects a new assessment, even as other analysts have shown varied ratings and price target changes for the data center REIT in recent months. The average target price from 25 analysts is $194.60, implying a 23.41% upside from its current price, with the consensus brokerage recommendation being "Outperform".

feed.title: Mizuho Cuts Digital Realty Trust (NYSE:DLR) Price Target to $180.00
feed.summary: Mizuho has adjusted its price target for Digital Realty Trust (NYSE:DLR) from $191 to $180, while maintaining an "outperform" rating, indicating a potential 14.65% upside. The consensus among analysts remains a "Moderate Buy" with an average price target of $194.10. Despite the price target cut, institutional investors hold a significant 99.7% of the stock, and the company's shares are currently trading around $157.01, below the average analyst target.

feed.title: Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) Price Target Raised to $152.00 at Mizuho
feed.summary: Mizuho has increased its price target for Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) from $150.00 to $152.00, maintaining a "neutral" rating and suggesting a nearly 9.9% upside. Other analysts have also adjusted their targets, contributing to a "Moderate Buy" consensus with an average price target of $146.94. The company's stock recently traded at $138.32, with insider sales totaling approximately 86,094 shares valued at about $11.96 million in the last 90 days.

feed.title: Is P&G (PG) a Buy as Wall Street Analysts Look Optimistic?
feed.summary: This article analyzes Procter & Gamble's (PG) investment potential based on Wall Street analyst recommendations versus Zacks Rank. While analysts show optimism for PG with an average brokerage recommendation (ABR) leaning towards "Strong Buy," the article highlights potential biases in these recommendations. It advocates for using the Zacks Rank, which is based on earnings estimate revisions, as a more reliable indicator for future stock price movements, noting that PG currently holds a Zacks Rank #4 (Sell) due to declining earnings estimates.

feed.title: iRhythm Technologies stock maintains Overweight rating at Morgan Stanley
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has maintained an Overweight rating and $205.00 price target on iRhythm Technologies (NASDAQ:IRTC) following strong preliminary fourth-quarter results, which exceeded consensus revenue estimates. The company reported robust year-over-year revenue growth and provided an optimistic outlook for fiscal year 2026, surpassing Street estimates, despite not yet expecting profitability this year. The forecast also does not include potential revenue from its next-generation Mobile Cardiac Telemetry device, which awaits FDA clearance and could significantly increase market share.

feed.title: American Tower (NYSE:AMT) Price Target Cut to $189.00 by Analysts at Mizuho
feed.summary: Mizuho analysts have lowered their price target for American Tower (NYSE:AMT) from $217.00 to $189.00, maintaining a "neutral" rating, though the consensus among analysts remains a "Moderate Buy" with an average target of $223.32. The real estate investment trust recently reported earnings of $2.78 per share, exceeding analyst estimates, and traded up 1.0% during midday trading on Monday. Insider and institutional activity includes a significant share sale by Tower Corp and purchases by a director, with institutional investors owning 92.69% of the stock.

feed.title: Zacks Market Edge Highlights: Exxon Mobil, Chevron and ConocoPhillips
feed.summary: The Zacks Market Edge podcast, hosted by Tracey Ryniec, discusses whether oil stocks like Exxon Mobil (XOM), Chevron (CVX), and ConocoPhillips (COP) are values or traps for 2026, especially considering recent developments in Venezuela and consistently low oil prices. The analysis delves into the earnings outlook and financial metrics for each company, noting expected declines in earnings for 2025 and 2026 despite some stock price increases. The podcast aims to help investors determine if these major oil players are attractive investments given their current valuations and future prospects.

feed.title: Biogen Receives European Commission Approval for High Dose Regimen of SPINRAZA® (nusinersen) for Spinal Muscular Atrophy
feed.summary: Biogen has received European Commission marketing authorization for a high-dose regimen of SPINRAZA® (nusinersen) for the treatment of 5q spinal muscular atrophy (SMA). This approval is based on data from the DEVOTE study, which demonstrated improved motor function in both treatment-naïve and previously-treated SMA patients. The new regimen includes a more rapid loading phase and 28 mg maintenance doses every four months, offering an important advancement in SMA care.

feed.title: Higher-dose spinal muscular atrophy treatment approved for patients in EU
feed.summary: The European Commission has approved Biogen's high-dose SPINRAZA (nusinersen) regimen for 5q spinal muscular atrophy, the most common form of the disease. This updated dosing schedule involves two 50 mg loading doses followed by 28 mg maintenance injections every four months, supported by positive data from the DEVOTE Phase 2/3 study. The new regimen aims to address the evolving needs of SMA patients and is also under review by the U.S. FDA with a decision expected by April 3, 2026.

feed.title: Coca-Cola's Innovation Pipeline: Catalyst for Volume Acceleration?
feed.summary: The Coca-Cola Company's innovation strategy, focusing on product, packaging, digital/AI, and sustainability, is crucial for its long-term growth and market leadership. Despite these efforts, KO is facing soft volumes in key markets due to consumer strain and changing category trends, particularly a 1% drop in the Asia Pacific region in Q3 2025. While Coca-Cola relies on price/mix gains, innovation in zero-sugar and flavor-led offerings, combined with productivity and cost control, is expected to help stabilize and increase volumes and profitability. Competitors like PepsiCo and Monster Beverage are also heavily investing in innovation to meet evolving consumer demands.

feed.title: 5 takeaways from Xcel CEO Bob Frenzel’s talk at the Minneapolis Fed
feed.summary: Xcel Energy CEO Bob Frenzel discussed the dramatic impact of data center growth on electric utilities at the Minneapolis Fed, highlighting multiple drivers of demand growth, degrading supply chains, and the need for alternative rate structures. He also advocated for nuclear energy as a preferred source for data centers and noted the significant rise in engineering, procurement, and construction costs due to competition from hyperscalers. The utility is adapting its strategies to manage this evolving landscape, including long-term contracts and advocating for national initiatives in nuclear power.

feed.title: Medtronic wins FDA clearance for MiniMed Go app for InPen smart insulin pen
feed.summary: Medtronic has received FDA 510(k) clearance for its MiniMed Go app, designed for multiple daily injections (MDI). This smart MDI system integrates the InPen smart insulin pen with Abbott's Instinct sensor, providing real-time personalized insights and guidance for users. The collaboration between Medtronic and Abbott, initiated in August 2024, aims to enhance insulin delivery and continuous glucose monitoring technologies, with the MiniMed Go app offering features like missed dose alerts and an integrated dose calculator.

feed.title: Peachtree refinances Atlanta hotel
feed.summary: Peachtree Group has refinanced the 265-key Candler Hotel in Atlanta, a property operating under Hilton's Curio Collection and managed by Highgate Hotels. JLL Hotels & Hospitality facilitated the transaction for the hotel, which was originally built in 1906 by Coca-Cola magnate Asa Griggs Candler and is listed on the National Register of Historic Places. The refinancing reflects lender confidence in the hotel's market position and Atlanta's recovery as a convention destination.
